answer:
The degree of \( f(g(x)) \) is \( 6 \cdot 3 = 18 \), and the degree of \( g(h(x)) \) is \( 3 \cdot \deg(h) \).  
For the sum to have degree 36, the highest-degree term must come from \( h(f(x)) \), whose degree is \( \deg(h) \cdot 6 \).  
Thus \( 6 \cdot \deg(h) = 36 \), so \( \deg(h) = 6 \).

\boxed{C}
